name: DES-4B "Worldeater"
description: The pinnacle of deep efficiency sifting technology, the Worldeater processes 100 tonnes of raw regolith per hour through a fully integrated plasma-magnetic extraction line. A high-density plasma pre-treatment stage operates at elevated power to decompose not just surface-adsorbed water but also hydroxyl groups chemically bound into mineral crystal lattices — liberating water that no purely mechanical or thermal process can reach. Four superconducting bores run in parallel with adaptive field shaping that continuously optimizes separation trajectories based on real-time particle composition feedback from embedded spectroscopic sensors. The cryocondenser array is sized for peak vapor loads and includes a fractional distillation column that can separate water isotopologues if required (HDO, D₂O) for deuterium recovery. At 270 tonnes and 22 MW, the Worldeater represents the upper limit of what can reasonably be called 'equipment' rather than 'infrastructure', and its deployment typically anchors a major permanent settlement. Water output of 2,347 kg/hr — nearly 1,700 tonnes per game month — can supply propellant depots, life support systems, and industrial feedstock for an entire regional base network.
